heading: PAYGO estimates and PAYGO scorecards
label: 933 PAYGO estimates and PAYGO scorecards

children.heading: House of Representatives
children.label: A House of Representatives
children.text: To establish the budgetary effects of a PAYGO Act consistent with the determination made by the Chairman of the House Budget Committee, a PAYGO Act originated in or amended by the House of Representatives may include the following statement: “The budgetary effects of this Act, for the purpose of complying with the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go-Act of 2010, shall be determined by reference to the latest statement titled ‘Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation’ for this Act, submitted for printing in the Congressional Record by the Chairman of the House Budget Committee, provided that such statement has been submitted prior to the vote on passage.”.1So in original. Probably should be “Pay-As-You-Go Act”.1

children.heading: Senate
children.label: B Senate
children.text: To establish the budgetary effects of a PAYGO Act consistent with the determination made by the Chairman of the Senate Budget Committee, a PAYGO Act originated in or amended by the Senate shall include the following statement: “The budgetary effects of this Act, for the purpose of complying with the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go-Act of 2010, shall be determined by reference to the latest statement titled ‘Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation’ for this Act, submitted for printing in the Congressional Record by the Chairman of the Senate Budget Committee, provided that such statement has been submitted prior to the vote on passage.”.1

children.heading: Conference reports and amendments between the Houses
children.label: C Conference reports and amendments between the Houses
children.text: To establish the budgetary effects of the conference report on a PAYGO Act, or an amendment to an amendment between Houses on a PAYGO Act, which if estimated shall be estimated jointly by the Chairmen of the House and Senate Budget Committees, the conference report or amendment between the Houses shall include the following statement: “The budgetary effects of this Act, for the purpose of complying with the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go-Act of 2010, shall be determined by reference to the latest statement titled ‘Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation’ for this Act, jointly submitted for printing in the Congressional Record by the Chairmen of the House and Senate Budget Committees, provided that such statement has been submitted prior to the vote on passage in the House acting first on this conference report or amendment between the Houses.”.1

children.heading: Determination of budgetary effects of PAYGO Acts
children.label: 2 Determination of budgetary effects of PAYGO Acts

children.heading: Statement and estimate
children.label: i Statement and estimate
children.text: Prior to a vote on passage of a PAYGO Act originated or amended by one House, the Chairman of the Budget Committee of that House may submit for printing in the Congressional Record a statement titled “Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation” which shall include an estimate of the budgetary effects of that Act, if available prior to passage of the Act by that House and shall submit, if applicable, an identification of any current policy adjustments made pursuant to . The timely submission of such a statement, in conjunction with the appropriate designation made pursuant to paragraph (1)(A) or (1)(B), as applicable, shall establish the budgetary effects of the PAYGO Act for the purposes of this Act.section 936 of this title

children.heading: Effect
children.label: ii Effect
children.text: The latest statement submitted by the Chairman of the Budget Committee of that House prior to passage shall supersede any prior statements submitted in the Congressional Record and shall be valid only if the PAYGO Act is not further amended by either House.

children.label: I
children.text: the estimate required by clause (i) has not been submitted prior to passage by that House;

children.label: II
children.text: such estimate has been submitted but is no longer valid due to a subsequent amendment to the PAYGO Act; or

children.label: III
children.text: the designation required pursuant to this subsection has not been made;

children.heading: Conference reports and amendments between Houses
children.label: B Conference reports and amendments between Houses

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Prior to the adoption of a report of a committee of conference on a PAYGO Act in either House, or disposition of an amendment to an amendment between Houses on a PAYGO Act, the Chairmen of the Budget Committees of the House and Senate may jointly submit for printing in the Congressional Record a statement titled “Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation” which shall include an estimate of the budgetary effects of that Act if available prior to passage of the Act by the House acting first on the legislation and shall submit, if applicable, an identification of any current policy adjustments made pursuant to . The timely submission of such a statement, in conjunction with the appropriate designation made pursuant to paragraph (1)(C), shall establish the budgetary effects of the PAYGO Act for the purposes of this Act.section 936 of this title

children.heading: Failure to submit estimate
children.label: ii Failure to submit estimate
children.text: If such estimate has not been submitted prior to the adoption of a report of a committee of conference by either House, or if the designation required pursuant to this subsection has not been made, the budgetary effects of the PAYGO Act shall be determined under subsection (d)(3).

children.heading: Procedure in the Senate
children.label: 3 Procedure in the Senate
children.text: In the Senate, upon submission of a statement titled “Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation” by the Chairman of the Senate Budget Committee for printing in the Congressional Record, the Legislative Clerk shall read the statement.

children.heading: Jurisdiction of the Budget Committees
children.label: 4 Jurisdiction of the Budget Committees
children.text: For the purposes of enforcing , a designation made pursuant to paragraph (1)(A), (1)(B), or (1)(C), that includes only the language specifically prescribed therein, shall not be considered a matter within the jurisdiction of either the Senate or House Committees on the Budget.section 637 of this title

children.heading: Current policy adjustments for certain legislation
children.label: c Current policy adjustments for certain legislation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For any provision of legislation that meets the criteria in subsection (c), (d), (e) or (f) of , the Chairs of the Committees on the Budget of the House and Senate, as applicable, shall request that CBO adjust the estimate of budgetary effects of that legislation pursuant to paragraph (2) for the purposes of this chapter. A single piece of legislation may contain provisions that meet criteria in more than one of the subsections referred to in the preceding sentence. CBO shall adjust estimates for legislation designated under subsection (a) and estimated under subsection (b). OMB shall adjust estimates for legislation estimated under subsection (d)(3).section 936 of this title

children.heading: Estimates
children.label: A Estimates
children.text: CBO or OMB, as applicable, shall exclude from the estimate of budgetary effects any budgetary effects of a provision that meets the criteria in subsection (c), (d), (e) or (f) of , to the extent that those budgetary effects, when combined with all other excluded budgetary effects of any other previously designated provisions of enacted legislation under the same subsection of , do not exceed the maximum applicable current policy adjustment defined under the applicable subsection of for the applicable 10-year period.section 936 of this titlesection 936 of this titlesection 936 of this title

children.heading: Baseline
children.label: B Baseline
children.text: Any estimate made pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be prepared using baseline estimates supplied by the Congressional Budget Office, consistent with . CBO estimates of legislation adjusted for current policy shall include a separate presentation of costs excluded from the calculation of budgetary effects for the legislation, as well as an updated total of all excluded costs of provisions within subsection (c), (d), or (e) of , as applicable, and in the case of paragraph (1) of , within any of the subparagraphs (A) through (L) of such paragraph, as applicable.section 907 of this titlesection 936 of this titlesection 936(f) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on availability of excess savings
children.label: 3 Limitation on availability of excess savings

children.heading: Prohibition on use of excess saving for ineligible policies2So in original. Probably should be “savings”.2
children.label: A Prohibition on use of excess saving for ineligible policies2So in original. Probably should be “savings”.2
children.text: To the extent the adjustment for current policy of any provision estimated under this subsection exceeds the estimated budgetary effects of that provision, these excess savings shall not be available to offset the costs of any provisions not otherwise eligible for a current policy adjustment under , and shall not be counted on the PAYGO scorecards established pursuant to subsections (d)(4) and (d)(5).section 936 of this title

children.heading: Prohibition on use of excess savings across budget areas
children.label: B Prohibition on use of excess savings across budget areas
children.text: For provisions eligible for a current policy adjustment under subsections (c) through (f) of , to the extent the adjustment for current policy of any provision exceeds the estimated budgetary effects of that same provision, the excess savings shall be available only to offset the costs of other provisions that qualify for a current policy adjustment in that same subsection. Each paragraph in shall be considered a separate subsection for purposes of this section.section 936 of this titlesection 936(f)(1) of this title

children.heading: Further guidance on estimating budgetary effects
children.label: 4 Further guidance on estimating budgetary effects
children.text: Estimates of budgetary effects under this subsection shall be consistent with the guidance provided at .section 936(h) of this title

children.heading: Inclusion of statement
children.label: 5 Inclusion of statement
children.text: For PAYGO legislation adjusted pursuant to , the Chairman of the House or Senate Budget Committee, as applicable, shall include in any statement titled “Budgetary Effects of PAYGO Legislation”, submitted for that legislation pursuant to this section, an explanation of the current policy designation and adjustments.section 936 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: OMB shall maintain and make publicly available a continuously updated document containing two PAYGO scorecards displaying the budgetary effects of PAYGO legislation as determined under , applying the look-back requirement in subsection (e) and the averaging requirement in subsection (f), and a separate addendum displaying the estimates of the costs of provisions designated in statute as emergency requirements.section 639 of this title

children.heading: Estimates in legislation
children.label: 2 Estimates in legislation
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), in making the calculations for the PAYGO scorecards, OMB shall use the budgetary effects included by reference in the applicable legislation pursuant to subsection (a).

children.heading: OMB PAYGO estimates
children.label: 3 OMB PAYGO estimates
children.text: If a PAYGO Act does not contain a valid reference to its budgetary effects consistent with subsection (a), OMB shall estimate the budgetary effects of that legislation upon its enactment. The OMB estimate shall be based on the approaches to scorekeeping set forth in , as amended by this title, and subsection (g)(4), and shall use the same economic and technical assumptions as used in the most recent budget submitted by the President under .section 639 of this title3section 1105(a) of title 31See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: 5-year scorecard
children.label: 4 5-year scorecard
children.text: The first scorecard shall display the budgetary effects of PAYGO legislation in each year over the 5-year period beginning in the budget year.

children.heading: 10-year scorecard
children.label: 5 10-year scorecard
children.text: The second scorecard shall display the budgetary effects of PAYGO legislation in each year over the 10-year period beginning in the budget year.

children.heading: Community Living Assistance Services and Supports Act
children.label: 6 Community Living Assistance Services and Supports Act
children.text: Neither scorecard maintained by OMB pursuant to this subsection shall include net savings from any provisions of legislation titled “Community Living Assistance Services and Supports Act”, which establishes a Federal insurance program for long-term care, if such legislation is enacted into law, or amended, subsequent to .February 12, 2010

children.heading: Look-back to capture current-year effects
children.label: e Look-back to capture current-year effects
children.text: For purposes of this section, OMB shall treat the budgetary effects of PAYGO legislation enacted during a session of Congress that occur during the current year as though they occurred in the budget year.

children.heading: Averaging used to measure compliance over 5-year and 10-year periods
children.label: f Averaging used to measure compliance over 5-year and 10-year periods

children.text: for purposes of the 5-year scorecard referred to in subsection (d)(4), the four subsequent outyears, divide that cumulative total by five, and enter the quotient in the budget-year column and in each subsequent column of the 5-year PAYGO scorecard; and

for purposes of the 10-year scorecard referred to in subsection (d)(5), the nine subsequent outyears, divide that cumulative total by ten, and enter the quotient in the budget-year column and in each subsequent column of the 10-year PAYGO scorecard.

children.heading: Designation in statute
children.label: 1 Designation in statute
children.text: If a provision of direct spending or revenue legislation in a PAYGO Act is enacted as an emergency requirement that the Congress so designates in statute pursuant to this section, the amounts of new budget authority, outlays, and revenue in all fiscal years resulting from that provision shall be treated as an emergency requirement for the purposes of this Act.

children.heading: Designation in the House of Representatives
children.label: 2 Designation in the House of Representatives
children.text: If a PAYGO Act includes a provision expressly designated as an emergency for the purposes of this chapter, the Chair shall put the question of consideration with respect thereto.

children.heading: Point of order in the Senate
children.label: 3 Point of order in the Senate

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: When the Senate is considering a PAYGO Act, if a point of order is made by a Senator against an emergency designation in that measure, that provision making such a designation shall be stricken from the measure and may not be offered as an amendment from the floor.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: i Waiver
children.text: Subparagraph (A) may be waived or suspended in the Senate only by an affirmative vote of three-fifths of the Members, duly chosen and sworn.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: ii Appeals
children.text: Appeals in the Senate from the decisions of the Chair relating to any provision of this subsection shall be limited to 1 hour, to be equally divided between, and controlled by, the appellant and the manager of the bill or joint resolution, as the case may be. An affirmative vote of three-fifths of the Members of the Senate, duly chosen and sworn, shall be required to sustain an appeal of the ruling of the Chair on a point of order raised under this subsection.

children.heading: Definition of an emergency designation
children.label: C Definition of an emergency designation
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a provision shall be considered an emergency designation if it designates any item as an emergency requirement pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: Form of the point of order
children.label: D Form of the point of order
children.text: A point of order under subparagraph (A) may be raised by a Senator as provided in .section 644(e) of this title

children.heading: Conference reports
children.label: E Conference reports
children.text: When the Senate is considering a conference report on, or an amendment between the Houses in relation to, a PAYGO Act, upon a point of order being made by any Senator pursuant to this section, and such point of order being sustained, such material contained in such conference report shall be deemed stricken, and the Senate shall proceed to consider the question of whether the Senate shall recede from its amendment and concur with a further amendment, or concur in the House amendment with a further amendment, as the case may be, which further amendment shall consist of only that portion of the conference report or House amendment, as the case may be, not so stricken. Any such motion in the Senate shall be debatable. In any case in which such point of order is sustained against a conference report (or Senate amendment derived from such conference report by operation of this subsection), no further amendment shall be in order.

children.heading: Effect of designation on scoring
children.label: 4 Effect of designation on scoring
children.text: If a provision is designated as an emergency requirement under this Act, CBO or OMB, as applicable, shall not include the budgetary effects of such a provision in its estimate of the budgetary effects of that PAYGO legislation.
